2605.01203.title: GR-Ben: A General Reasoning Benchmark for Evaluating Process Reward Models
2605.01203.paper_summary: Currently, process reward models (PRMs) have exhibited remarkable potential for test-time scaling. Since large language models (LLMs) regularly generate flawed intermediate reasoning steps when tackling a broad spectrum of reasoning and decision-making tasks, PRMs are required to possess capabilities for detecting process-level errors in real-world scenarios. However, existing benchmarks primarily focus on mathematical reasoning, thereby failing to comprehensively evaluate the error detection ability of PRMs across diverse reasoning scenarios. To mitigate this gap, we introduce GR-Ben, a process-level benchmark specifically designed for assessing PRM's performance across two primary reasoning domains (science and logic) and nine subdomains. We conduct extensive experiments on a diverse set of 22 models, encompassing both PRMs and LLMs, and derive two key findings: (1) In domains beyond mathematical reasoning, the error-detection ability of existing PRMs and LLMs is found to be markedly weaker by comparison.(2) In general, PRMs are less adept at identifying knowledge-based errors, whereas LLMs exhibit poorer performance in detecting computational errors.We hope GR-Ben can foster future researches on PRMs for general domains, thereby enhancing the reasoning capabilities of LLMs.
2605.01203.paper_summary_zh: 目前，過程獎勵模型（PRMs）在測試時間擴展方面展現了顯著的潛力。由於大型語言模型（LLMs）在處理廣泛的推理和決策任務時，經常會生成有缺陷的中間推理步驟，因此需要PRMs具備在現實場景中檢測過程級錯誤的能力。然而，現有的基準主要集中於數學推理，因而未能全面評估PRMs在多樣推理場景中的錯誤檢測能力。為了填補這一空白，我們推出了GR-Ben，一個專門設計用於評估PRM在兩個主要推理領域（科學和邏輯）及九個子領域中的表現的過程級基準。我們對22個多樣化模型進行了廣泛實驗，涵蓋了PRMs和LLMs，並得出了兩個關鍵發現：（1）在超出數學推理的領域中，現有PRMs和LLMs的錯誤檢測能力相比之下明顯較弱。（2）一般而言，PRMs在識別基於知識的錯誤方面較不擅長，而LLMs在檢測計算錯誤方面表現較差。我們希望GR-Ben能促進未來對於一般領域PRMs的研究，從而增強LLMs的推理能力。
2605.01203.author: Zhouhao Sun et.al.
2605.01203.authors: Zhouhao Sun, Xuan Zhang, Xiao Ding, Bibo Cai, Li Du, Kai Xiong, Xinran Dai, Fei Zhang, weidi tang, Zhiyuan Kan, Yang Zhao, Bing Qin, Ting Liu
